proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous predictor feature_078 is positively associated with pfs_months (higher values of feature_078 predict longer progression-free survival).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.766 (p < 1e-300). PFS increases monotonically across feature_078 quintiles: 1.86, 3.33, 4.30, 5.27, 6.83 months from lowest to highest quintile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0 (negative main effect of feature_051).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 5.05 mo when feature_051=0 (n=22519) vs 3.70 mo when feature_051=1 (n=27481). Welch t = -68.5, p < 1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 is an ordered three-level categorical variable showing a monotonic decrease in mean pfs_months from level 0 to level 2.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean(); from scipy import stats; stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_057']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_057=0 -> 5.27 mo (n=17592); =1 -> 4.05 mo (n=24971); =2 -> 2.91 mo (n=7437). One-way ANOVA F=3528.9, p < 1e-300; the effect is dose-response (monotonic).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_038 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038 = 0 (positive main effect of feature_038, plausibly a treatment indicator).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_038']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_038']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.12 mo when feature_038=0 (n=39978) vs 5.09 mo when feature_038=1 (n=10022). Welch t = 32.0, p ~ 1.7e-216.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 0 to ~25) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5 to 5.5, mean ~3.8) is positively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_099'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.123, p ~ 2.2e-167; supports negative association of feature_099 with PFS.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_092'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.102, p ~ 3.2e-115; supports positive association of feature_092 with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_013 = 0.
Patients with feature_043 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_043 = 0.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_013']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_013']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.45 mo when feature_013=0 (n=29102) vs 4.12 mo when feature_013=1 (n=20898). t = -16.1, p ~ 1.9e-58.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_043']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_043']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.42 mo when feature_043=0 (n=32343) vs 4.11 mo when feature_043=1 (n=17657). t = -14.8, p ~ 1.0e-49.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_109 = 1 (a low-prevalence binary, ~4.5%) have shorter pfs_months than feature_109 = 0 patients.
Patients with feature_067 = 1 (a low-prevalence binary, ~3%) have longer pfs_months than feature_067 = 0 patients in unadjusted analysis.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_109']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_109']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_067']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_067']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the strongest predictors (feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, feature_038), the categorical variable feature_064 (race/ethnicity: white, black, hispanic, asian, other) has no statistically significant effect on pfs_months — i.e., apparent disparities in PFS are explained by clinical/biomarker confounders.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_064) + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All four C(feature_064) contrasts (black, hispanic, other, white vs asian reference) had |coef| < 0.05 mo and p > 0.37 after adjustment; partial F p > 0.5. No PFS difference by feature_064 remains after adjusting for clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical predictors, feature_018 (insurance: medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured) is not significantly associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_018) + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All three C(feature_018) contrasts (medicare, private, uninsured vs medicaid reference) had |coef| < 0.04 mo with p > 0.11. No significant adjusted effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on the leading univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_051, C(feature_057), feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, feature_009, feature_109, feature_067), each predictor retains its expected sign with high statistical significance — except feature_067 which loses significance once feature_038 is in the model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_067', data=df).fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between feature_038 (treatment-like) and feature_057 (3-level severity-like): the positive effect of feature_038 on pfs_months attenuates as feature_057 increases from 0 to 2.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * C(feature_057) + feature_078 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Main effect feature_038 = +0.984 mo at feature_057=0; interaction feature_038:C(feature_057)[1] = -0.066 (p=0.006); feature_038:C(feature_057)[2] = -0.112 (p=0.001). Stratified estimates: feature_038 effect = 0.99, 0.92, 0.88 mo at feature_057 = 0, 1, 2 respectively. Modest but real attenuation with severity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 modifies the feature_038 effect: in feature_043=0 patients the positive effect of feature_038 is large, but in feature_043=1 patients there is essentially no benefit from feature_038 (predictive-biomarker pattern of resistance).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_043 + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit(); subgroup OLS within strata.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_038:feature_043 = -1.434 mo, p ~ 0. Stratified: feature_043=0 -> feature_038 effect = +1.44 mo (p<1e-300, n=32343); feature_043=1 -> feature_038 effect = +0.002 mo (p=0.82, n=17657). Treatment benefit is entirely abolished in feature_043=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 modifies the feature_038 effect: in feature_013=0 patients there is a large positive feature_038 effect, but in feature_013=1 patients there is no feature_038 effect (second predictive resistance biomarker).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_013 + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit(); subgroup OLS within strata.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_038:feature_013 = -1.625 mo, p ~ 0. Stratified: feature_013=0 -> feature_038 effect = +1.62 mo (p<1e-300, n=29102); feature_013=1 -> feature_038 effect = -0.008 mo (p=0.30, n=20898).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 also abolishes the feature_038 benefit: in feature_109=1 patients there is no positive effect of feature_038 on PFS (third predictive resistance biomarker, low prevalence).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_109 + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit(); subgroup OLS within strata.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_038:feature_109 = -0.974 mo, p ~ 6e-80. Stratified: feature_109=0 -> feature_038 effect = +0.98 mo (p<1e-300, n=47728); feature_109=1 -> feature_038 effect = +0.006 mo (p=0.78, n=2272).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (low-prevalence, ~3%) is a positive predictive biomarker for feature_038: in feature_067=1 patients the feature_038 PFS benefit is roughly double that of feature_067=0 patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_067 * feature_038 + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit(); subgroup OLS within strata.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_067:feature_038 = +0.912 mo, p ~ 1e-39. Stratified: feature_067=0 -> feature_038 effect = +0.91 mo (p<1e-300, n=48494); feature_067=1 -> feature_038 effect = +1.82 mo (p ~ 4e-109, n=1506). The feature_067 main effect (-0.02 mo, p=0.51) is null after adjustment; effect is purely modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients negative for ALL three resistance markers (feature_043=0 AND feature_013=0 AND feature_109=0), feature_038 confers a markedly larger PFS benefit than the cohort-wide average; among patients positive for ANY of these three markers, feature_038 confers no benefit.

analyses.code: df['any_resistance'] = ((df.feature_043==1)|(df.feature_013==1)|(df.feature_109==1)).astype(int); subgroup OLS by any_resistance.
analyses.result_summary: Resistance-negative (n=17372, 35% of cohort): adjusted feature_038 effect = +2.71 mo (p<1e-300). Resistance-positive (n=32628, 65% of cohort): adjusted feature_038 effect = -0.003 mo (p=0.65). Joint marker cleanly separates responders from non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 and feature_013 are statistically independent (each is a distinct marker rather than redundant): chi-square test of independence yields p > 0.5; both independently abolish feature_038 benefit when present.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_013'], df['feature_043'])); subgroup OLS in 2x2 strata.
analyses.result_summary: Chi-square = 0.27, p = 0.60 (independence not rejected). Joint subgroup feature_038 effects: (043=0,013=0) +2.49 mo (p<1e-300, n=18853); (043=0,013=1) -0.008 mo (p=0.40, n=13490); (043=1,013=0) +0.009 mo (p=0.43, n=10249); (043=1,013=1) -0.007 mo (p=0.55, n=7408). Either marker alone is sufficient to abolish benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 is mutually exclusive with feature_013 (no patient has both feature_109=1 and feature_013=1): suggests they may represent overlapping/exclusive molecular alterations.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_109'], df['feature_013'])
analyses.result_summary: Cross-tab shows 0 patients with feature_109=1 and feature_013=1 (vs ~948 expected under independence). Strong negative association — these alterations are mutually exclusive in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 modifies the feature_051 main effect: the negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is amplified at higher feature_078 values (negative interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_051 + feature_038 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078:feature_051 = -0.0070, p ~ 2e-15. Per-unit-feature_078 PFS gain is +0.180 mo when feature_051=0 vs +0.173 mo when feature_051=1. Small but real attenuation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 modifies the feature_038 effect: the feature_038 PFS benefit is somewhat smaller in patients with high feature_092 than in patients with low feature_092.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_092 * feature_038 + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit(); median split subgroup analysis.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_092:feature_038 = -0.091, p ~ 2e-5. Median-split: feature_092 below median -> feature_038 effect = +0.98 mo; feature_092 above median -> +0.89 mo. Statistically significant but small in magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, and feature_057, feature_064 (race) does not modify the feature_038 effect — i.e., no race-by-treatment interaction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * C(feature_064) + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All four feature_038:C(feature_064) interaction contrasts had p > 0.14 (smallest p = 0.14 for feature_038:C(feature_064)[other], coef = +0.13). No evidence of race-modified treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a three-way interaction feature_038 x feature_051 x feature_057: in feature_057=1 patients, the feature_038 effect is more attenuated by feature_051=1 than in feature_057=0 patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_051 * C(feature_057) + feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038:feature_051:C(feature_057)[1] = +0.172, p ~ 3e-4 (significant); feature_038:feature_051:C(feature_057)[2] = -0.061, p = 0.37 (NS). Two-way feature_038:feature_051 alone is NS (p=0.19). Three-way modulation exists between feature_057 levels 0 and 1 only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 is a continuous variable with a small but highly significant negative association with pfs_months that persists after adjustment for all leading predictors.
feature_006 is a continuous variable (range 48-830, mean 224) with a small negative adjusted effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_009 + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_009 coef = -0.0050 mo per unit, p ~ 4e-78. Small in magnitude given values 0-30+, but unambiguously significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_006 + feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_006 coef = -0.00043 mo per unit, p ~ 1.3e-15. Per 100-unit increase: -0.043 mo. Small negative adjusted effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most of the 80 binary features beyond the seven already tested (feature_038, feature_051, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_067) and the 35 continuous features beyond the four already tested have NO significant adjusted effect on pfs_months — i.e., the strongest signal in this dataset is concentrated in a small number of variables.

analyses.code: Compute residuals from base model ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 and run univariate t-tests / pearson correlations of every other binary/continuous feature against the residual.
analyses.result_summary: After residualizing out the base model, only feature_013, feature_043, feature_092, feature_099, feature_109, feature_009, feature_067, feature_006 reach p < 1e-7 against the residual; almost all other features have p > 0.05 (out of >115 candidates tested). Signal concentrates in <12 features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining all the signals: (i) the cohort splits cleanly into a feature_038-responsive subgroup (defined by feature_043=0 AND feature_013=0 AND feature_109=0, which is ~35% of patients) where feature_038 confers ~+2.7 mo PFS gain, vs. a non-responsive subgroup where feature_038 is null; (ii) feature_067 further amplifies feature_038 benefit when present; (iii) feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, feature_092, feature_099 are prognostic regardless of feature_038 status.

analyses.code: Combine base model with biomarker-defined subgroups and feature_067 modifier.
analyses.result_summary: Joint conclusion supported: in feature_038-responsive (resistance-negative) subgroup, feature_038 effect = +2.71 mo (p<1e-300); in non-responsive subgroup, +0.0 mo (p=0.65). Adding feature_067 to the responsive subgroup further increases benefit (subgroup feature_067=1 within resistance-negative shows feature_038 effect ~+3.5 mo crude; not formally re-tested due to sparse cell). Prognostic continuous predictors (feature_078, feature_092, feature_099) and severity grade (feature_057) act independently of feature_038. Demographic variables (feature_064 race, feature_018 insurance) are NOT associated with PFS after adjustment, and do NOT modify treatment effect.
